verse_text: For the Father judges no one, but has committed all judgment to the Son,

links.context: Saints will judge the world (v. 2) and angels (v. 3) because in Christ, Christians share in His authority to judge (see [[MAT.19:28]]; [[JOH.5:22]], [[JOH.5:27]]; [[2PE.2:4]]; Jude 6; [[REV.20:4]]). This being so, how much more are we able to judge the everyday concerns of this life (v. 3)?

God executes judgment from the great white throne (see [[DAN.7:9]]; see also 1En 18:8). The One who sits on the throne in Revelation is God the Father (4:2-9; 5:1, 7, 13; 6:16; 7:10; etc.). However, in the rest of the NT Christ is the judge on the throne (Mt 25:31-46; [[JOH.5:22]]; [[ACT.10:42]]; 17:31; [[2CO.5:10]]). John is about to show their unity: "the throne of God and of the Lamb" (22:1). Earth and heaven flee away ([[MAT.24:35]]; 2Pt 3:10-13) in terror, a cosmic sign of the great Day of the Lord and the coming of the new heaven and earth (21:1).

Ps 71 is a prophecy concerning Solomon, who is Christ Himself, the King of Peace, for the name "Solomon" means "peace." And the Father (God) gave all judgments to Him (v. 1; see also [[JOH.5:22]]), and in the world to come (forever and unto ages of ages), all the earth shall be filled with His glory (v. 19).
